message:
fix(core+cli): reap killed children to avoid zombies; count real ref total in doctor orphan-refs scan

Three small correctness fixes that landed together.

git_cmd.rs — reap on try_wait error
------------------------------------
`wait_with_timeout(child, timeout)` handled three outcomes from
`child.try_wait()`: `Ok(Some(status))` (exited), `Ok(None)` (still
running — sleep and loop), and `Err(e)` (try_wait itself failed).
On the `Err` path we joined the stdout / stderr reader threads and
returned `GitRunOutcome::Error(e)`, but we did *not* reap the child.
`try_wait` failure is rare (EINTR, ECHILD, stale-handle) but when
it does happen we were leaving an unreaped child behind for the
rest of the server's lifetime. Added a best-effort
`let _ = child.kill(); let _ = child.wait();` before returning so
the PID is reclaimed. Both results are ignored because we already
have a fatal error in hand — no further signaling is useful.

git_binary.rs — reap on spawn-probe failure
--------------------------------------------
Symmetrical fix in `spawn_with_timeout`. On the read-side Err()
path we already called `child.kill()` before returning
`GitBinaryError::SpawnFailed`, but we never called `child.wait()`
to collect the exit status. Added the `wait()` so the child is
fully reaped before the error propagates up the stack.

doctor_orphan_refs.rs — real ref count in scan report
------------------------------------------------------
`scan_one_project` previously set `scanned_refs = findings.len()`
with a comment acknowledging that a proper total would need a
libgit2 walk. That produced confusing reports: the summary line
would show `0 findings / 0 scanned` for a clean repo and
`12 findings / 12 scanned` when 12 orphans were found, with no
visibility into the real denominator (say, 847 refs).

New `count_refs(project_path) -> Option<usize>` opens the repo
via `git2::Repository::open`, asks for `references()`, and counts
all flattened entries. Returns `None` on any libgit2 error; the
caller falls back to `findings.len()` so the reporter never
panics. With a non-None count, `DetectionSummary::from_findings`
now gets the true total and the CLI output reads `12 findings /
847 scanned`, which is actually useful.

Also cleaned up a clippy warning in the same function: the
`Some(format!("protected ref (main/master/HEAD); ..."))` call had
no interpolation, so `format!` was flagged as a useless allocation.
Replaced with a static `.to_string()` conversion.
